contaminant.name: PTFE Coating Residue
contaminant.description: Teflon-residue contamination, it arises from degraded fluoropolymer coatings during manufacturing processes and thus forms thin, persistent films on surfaces. This residue, it distinguishes itself by chemical inertness and low surface energy, unlike organic contaminants that degrade easily, and so resists adhesion on steel, aluminum, and stainless steel where it bonds tightly yet flakes under shear. Removal challenges include its thermal stability that demands precise energy input, but laser cleaning proves effective as pulsed beams ablate the layer selectively without substrate damage, and thus restores surface integrity.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbonyl Fluoride, Tetrafluoroethylene, Hexafluoropropylene - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Full-face respirator with appropriate cartridges, gas detection system, medical monitoring

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Enhanced ventilation required due to toxic gas generation - 15 ACH with HEPA+carbon
